did_this_paper_use_ai_to_obtain_results_.details: The paper uses an Improved K-means clustering algorithm optimized by the Lion Algorithm with New Mating Process (LANM) for segmentation, extracts features using Local Vector Pattern (LVP) and Grey-Level Co-Occurrence Matrix (GLCM), and employs a Deep Convolutional Neural Network (DCNN) for melanoma detection/classification.

is_this_paper_a_review_paper_or_a_resear.details: The paper proposes a novel melanoma detection model involving an Improved K-means clustering algorithm optimized by the Lion Algorithm with New Mating Process (LANM), feature extraction using Local Vector Pattern (LVP) and GLCM, and classification via Deep Convolutional Neural Network (DCNN). It presents original experimental results, including accuracy metrics (e.g., 0.86379 for normal skin images) and performance comparisons against conventional models using the PH2 and ISIC datasets, rather than summarizing prior literature.

title: A novel melanoma detection model: adapted K-means clustering-based segmentation process
brief_summary: This study proposes a novel melanoma detection model comprising segmentation, feature extraction, and classification phases to address the challenges of early skin cancer diagnosis. The segmentation phase utilizes an Improved K-means clustering algorithm optimized by the Lion Algorithm with New Mating Process (LANM) to select optimal centroids based on multi-objective constraints. Features are extracted using Local Vector Pattern (LVP) and Grey-Level Co-Occurrence Matrix (GLCM), which are then fed into a Deep Convolutional Neural Network (DCNN) for detection. The model's performance was evaluated on the PH2 and ISIC datasets using both holdout and tenfold cross-validation methods. Results indicated that the proposed framework achieved superior accuracy compared to conventional models, with an accuracy of 0.86379 for normal skin images on the PH2 dataset and 0.9095 on the ISIC dataset. The authors conclude that the proposed model is more robust for melanoma prediction than state-of-the-art techniques.

lesion_types_studied.details: Classified as normal skin or normal mole; described as generally evenly coloured black, tan or brown spot, round or oval shape, typically less than 6 mm. Used in PH2 and ISIC datasets for segmentation and detection evaluation.

Described as harmless, round or oval shape with uniform colour and contour. In PH2 dataset, includes 80 common nevi and 80 atypical nevi. Evolution from junctional to compound to intradermal nevi mentioned.
Described as malignant growth with mixed shades of tan, earthy and black colour. In PH2 dataset, includes 40 melanomas. Primary target of the detection model.

all_task_types.target: Melanoma detection (Normal vs. Benign vs. Malignant)
all_task_types.details: The paper proposes a melanoma detection model using a Deep Convolutional Neural Network (DCNN) to classify skin lesions into discrete diagnostic categories: Normal, Benign melanocytic lesions, and Malignant (Melanoma). Performance is evaluated using classification metrics such as accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, precision, F1-score, and MCC on the PH2 and ISIC datasets.

The model includes an Improved K-means clustering algorithm optimized by the Lion Algorithm with New Mating Process (LANM) for segmenting the dermoscopic images. Segmentation performance is evaluated using accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, precision, FPR, FNR, NPV, FDR, F1-score, and MCC.

datasets_used.name: PH2 Database
datasets_used.description: A dermoscopic image database obtained from the Dermatology Service of Hospital Pedro Hispano (Matosinhos, Portugal) using the Tuebinger Mole Analyser system. It contains 8-bit RGB colour images with a resolution of 768 × 560 pixels. The database includes medical annotation of all images, namely medical segmentation of the lesion, clinical and histological diagnosis, and the assessment of several dermoscopic criteria.

use.training: Used for training the proposed melanoma detection model (Improved K-means segmentation, LVP/GLCM feature extraction, DCNN classification) via holdout and tenfold cross-validation methods.
use.validation: Used for validation of segmentation and detection performance metrics (accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, etc.) via holdout and tenfold cross-validation methods.
use.testing: Used for testing the proposed model's performance against conventional models (MI, SVM, PSO + NN, CNN + SVM, PS-MSA) via holdout and tenfold cross-validation methods.

datasets_used.name: ISIC Database
datasets_used.description: An open-source public access archive of skin images developed by the International Skin Imaging Collaboration (ISIC) to test and validate standards for dermatologic imaging and automated diagnostic systems. It serves as a resource for teaching and development of automated diagnostic systems for melanoma.

use.training: Used for training the proposed melanoma detection model via holdout and tenfold cross-validation methods.
use.validation: Used for validation of segmentation and detection performance metrics (accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, etc.) via holdout and tenfold cross-validation methods.
use.testing: Used for testing the proposed model's performance against conventional models (MI, SVM, PSO + NN, CNN + SVM, PS-MSA) via holdout and tenfold cross-validation methods.

statistical_methods_used.purpose: Used for evaluating the performance of the proposed melanoma detection model on the PH2 and ISIC datasets.
Used for evaluating the performance of the proposed melanoma detection model on the PH2 and ISIC datasets.

hypothesis_testing.interpretation: The paper did not report formal hypothesis testing, p-values, or significance tests. Performance comparisons were made using descriptive metrics (accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, etc.) and percentage improvements without statistical significance testing.

components: Improved K-means clustering algorithm optimized by Lion Algorithm with New Mating Process (LANM) for segmentation
architecture_details.dcnn_description: Encapsulates diverse layers including convolutional, pooling, and fully connected layers. Uses convolution kernels to evaluate feature maps. Non-linearity introduced by activation function. Pooling layers perform downsampling. Output layer performs classification.

classification_or_output_head.function: Determines accurate results on the presence or absence of melanoma
classification_or_output_head.loss_function: Loss function defined as sum of differences between target labels and output over N input-output relations

machine_learning_or_deep_learning_models: Improved K-means clustering algorithm optimized by Lion Algorithm with New Mating Process (LANM)
preprocessing_techniques: Improved K-means clustering algorithm optimized by Lion Algorithm with New Mating Process (LANM) for segmentation
conclusions: The proposed melanoma detection model, which integrates an Improved K-means clustering algorithm optimized by the Lion Algorithm with New Mating Process (LANM) for segmentation, Local Vector Pattern (LVP) and Grey-Level Co-Occurrence Matrix (GLCM) for feature extraction, and a Deep Convolutional Neural Network (DCNN) for classification, is more robust for melanoma prediction compared to state-of-the-art models.

The segmentation phase using the proposed Improved K-means with LANM achieved superior accuracy compared to conventional K-means and PA-MSA, with an accuracy of 0.86379 for normal skin images on the PH2 dataset (47.83% better than conventional K-means) and 0.8964 on the ISIC dataset.

The overall detection framework achieved high classification accuracy, reaching 0.9375 on the PH2 dataset and 0.9256 on the ISIC dataset using tenfold cross-validation, outperforming conventional models such as MI, SVM, PSO + NN, CNN + SVM, and PS-MSA.
The proposed model demonstrated lower false negative rates (FNR) and false positive rates (FPR) in segmentation tasks compared to existing works, indicating enhanced robustness and error performance.
important_remarks: The study lacks formal statistical hypothesis testing (e.g., p-values, confidence intervals) to validate the significance of performance improvements over conventional models.
The PH2 dataset is small (200 images total: 80 common nevi, 80 atypical nevi, 40 melanomas), which may limit the generalizability and robustness of the deep learning model.
The ISIC dataset is used without specified class distribution or train/validation/test splits, raising concerns about data leakage or non-representative sampling.
The model was implemented in MATLAB, which is less common for deep learning research compared to frameworks like PyTorch or TensorFlow, potentially affecting reproducibility.
The proposed segmentation accuracy for abnormal images on the PH2 dataset is relatively low (0.75921), suggesting potential limitations in handling complex lesion boundaries.

The paper compares the proposed DCNN against older conventional models (MI, SVM, PSO+NN) rather than recent state-of-the-art deep learning architectures, which may overstate the relative performance gain.
No external validation on independent clinical datasets was performed; results are confined to PH2 and ISIC, limiting evidence of clinical applicability.
